column13: Incubated and operating out of Parvati/Electronic Co-Op Estate, Pune.
column15: Exceptionally high differentiation. Holds DSIR recognition (TU/IV-RD/4203) and deep IP for transforming silkworm proteins into tissue-regeneration scaffolds.
column17: Textbook "Tech DM" fit. Founded by active PhD research scientists from a premier national laboratory.

column21: Successfully launched their first commercial product (Seriderm) in Aug 2023 and recently raised ₹10 Cr in Seed funding (Aug 2024) to scale operations.
column23: Serigen is the ultimate "Federer" profile in terms of mindset and capability (PhD founders, deep IP, CSIR spin-off, high-tech biomedical products). They easily pass the Hard Kill rules (the founders still retain ~61% equity, so they are not VC-controlled). However, they fall drastically short on the revenue scale. With FY24 revenue under ₹1 Cr, they are a Seed-stage startup, not a mid-market established manufacturer (target ₹30-300Cr). If your mandate allows for early-stage deep-tech startups scaling up, they are a perfect target. If you need established cash flows, they are too small.
